title: Write an Ingress with TLS
description: Write an Ingress manifest with TLS termination and path-based routing to two backend Services.
starterCode:
# Write an Ingress manifest that:
# 1. apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1, kind: Ingress
# 2. Name: production-ingress, namespace: production
# 3. ingressClassName: nginx
# 4. TLS: host app.example.com, secretName: app-tls
# 5. Rules for host: app.example.com:
#    - /api (Prefix) -> api-service:8080
#    - / (Prefix) -> frontend-service:80
solutionCode:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: production-ingress
  namespace: production
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - app.example.com
      secretName: app-tls
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /api
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: api-service
                port:
                  number: 8080
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: frontend-service
                port:
                  number: 80
hints: TLS section references a Secret containing the TLS certificate
pathType: Prefix matches the path and any subpaths
Backend service uses service.name and service.port.number

validationRules.errorMessage: Set secretName for the TLS certificate
YAML is invalid — check Ingress spec indentation

simulatedOutput.success:
ingress.networking.k8s.io/production-ingress created

NAME                  CLASS   HOSTS             ADDRESS          PORTS     AGE
production-ingress    nginx   app.example.com   52.14.200.100    80, 443   10s

Routing:
  https://app.example.com/api  -> api-service:8080
  https://app.example.com/     -> frontend-service:80
  TLS: app-tls (app.example.com)
simulatedOutput.failure: Ingress validation failed. Check apiVersion (networking.k8s.io/v1), ingressClassName, TLS section, and path rules.
